# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.164.0/http/server.ts";
import { applyPlugins, Plugin } from "./plugin.ts";

interface Config {
  plugins?: string[];
}

const config: Config = {
  plugins: Deno.env.get("PLUGINS").split(",").map((p) => p.trim()),
};

async function main() {
  const handlers: Record<string, (req: Request) => Response> = {};

  for (const plugin of config.plugins || []) {
    try {
      const mod = await import(`${plugin}`);
      if ("default" in mod && typeof mod.default === "function") {
        handlers[mod.default.name] = mod.default.handler;
      }
    } catch (e) {
      console.error(`Failed to load plugin ${plugin}:`, e);
    }
  }

  serve(
    new Response("Hello, World!"),
    {
      port: 8080,
      handler: async (req: Request) => {
        const url = new URL(req.url!, "http://localhost");
        const handler = handlers[url.pathname];
        if (handler) {
          return handler(req);
        } else {
          return new Response("Not found", { status: 404 });
        }
      },
    }
  );
}

main();

// plugin.ts
export function applyPlugins(plugins: Plugin[]) {
  for (const plugin of plugins) {
    plugin.init();
  }
}

interface Plugin {
  name: string;
  init(): void;
  handler(req: Request): Response;
}
